refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the temporal scope (evaluation period), clarifying the requirement for measurable verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Monitor and log teleoperation link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; calculate availability and compare against the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from network link failures promptly.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and predictive failure analytics.
DP3: Employ adaptive bandwidth management and error correction protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundant communication paths logically isolate failure modes, minimizing coupling between network components and teleoperation control logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple network paths or technologies.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that failover latency may affect teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 as a complementary DP in future refinement to enhance predictive maintenance.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, including network link management and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data exchange and link status monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and health data to NGHE Solution for monitoring and logging.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as link availability logic is core to teleoperation; low diffusion as failover logic is centralized.
Vehicle module depends on link status but does not contain link availability logic.
Safety Services may receive alerts but does not implement link availability.
NGHE Solution oversees system-wide monitoring but does not implement failover.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability logic within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership and maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with NGHE Solution monitoring functions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate failover latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness and consider partial delegation of monitoring to NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network paths with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions ensuring link availability.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as failover logic is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link monitoring is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding predictive failure analytics in future refinement to complement redundancy.
Review integration points with Safety Services for alerting on link failures.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, incorporating predictive analytics and safety alert integration will strengthen the solution and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link uptime for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from network link failures promptly.

revise.why: To include complementary design parameters such as predictive failure analytics to enhance link availability robustness.

assumptions: Multiple network paths are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module can implement failover logic with acceptable latency.
open_risks: Failover latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
Integration complexity with system-wide monitoring and safety alerting.
evidence_needed: Verification results of link availability over evaluation period.
